issuesFound.explanation: The elements with IDs “dropdown‑trigger” and “dropdown‑menu” are likely non‑semantic (e.g., <div> or <span>) used as interactive controls. They lack an appropriate ARIA role (button for the trigger, menu for the menu) and the trigger lacks a tabindex, preventing keyboard‑only users from focusing and activating the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 64: const trigger = document.getElementById('dropdown‑trigger'); trigger.setAttribute('role', 'button'); trigger.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
  Line 65: const menu = document.getElementById('dropdown‑menu'); menu.setAttribute('role', 'menu');

issuesFound.title: Modal missing role="dialog"
issuesFound.explanation: The modal element is given aria‑modal but no role, so assistive technologies cannot identify it as a dialog window.
issuesFound.fix: Line 23: modal.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true'); modal.setAttribute('role', 'dialog');

issuesFound.title: Live region element missing aria-live attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element with ID “notification‑live” is used as a live region but does not have aria‑live (or role) defined, so screen readers may not announce updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 9: const live = document.getElementById('notification‑live'); live.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); live.setAttribute('role', 'status');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injection without ARIA live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: renderPage replaces the #app container’s innerHTML, changing visible page content without notifying assistive technologies, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new heading.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 102: app.innerHTML = `

${pageTitle}

`; const live = document.getElementById('page‑live'); if (live) { live.textContent = `Page loaded: ${pageTitle}`; }
